title: Pre-beta candidate refresh and operational readiness gate

preflight.note: Stamped by `repopact new`; the work item was recorded before implementation began.

acceptance_criteria.text: Assign a unique beta identity (for example 1.4.1-beta.1 or 1.5.0-beta.1) so package.json, VERSION, and Tauri agree, and expose that version plus a source/build identifier (commit SHA or equivalent) in diagnostic exports so testers cannot confuse this candidate with the June 22 1.4.0 installer.

Build and retain a Windows installer from the frozen candidate commit, recording full commit SHA, installer filename, SHA-256 checksum, file size, build environment, signed/unsigned status, content.revision, and application version/build ID; keep that exact artifact unchanged for the first pilot round.

Run local Windows gates (content, a11y, unit tests, frontend build, Rust fmt/check as applicable) and independently dispatch or otherwise obtain green remote Windows CI evidence against that exact candidate commit.

Complete a packaged-app acceptance pass on the exact beta installer covering clean install/SmartScreen, first-run tour, A+/Network+/Security+ track switch, lesson progress, domain-scoped Practice, PBQ types, short Mock plus weakest-domain remediation, Recall, notes/bookmarks, persistence across reopen, encrypted backup export/import, diagnostics identity/redaction, and uninstall/reinstall or upgrade data behavior.

Either complete an NVDA walkthrough of the beta candidate (tour, sidebar/Tools, track switcher, lessons, Practice, PBQ, Mock, Recall, backup/diagnostics, dialog focus) or explicitly scope the first beta as not yet assistive-technology-qualified and exclude AT users from the first cohort, recording the residual risk.

Publish a controlled pilot runbook covering Windows 10/11 x64 private cohort distribution, unsigned-installer/SmartScreen guidance, published checksum, tester consent/privacy expectations, support channel, known limitations, severity definitions, stop-the-pilot conditions, and feedback fields for app version, build ID, content.revision, track/exam/domain, and defect-vs-content distinction.

Reconcile WI 228 dependencies and roadmap/audit pointers so 228 depends on 310 and the actual July prerequisite chain (219–222, 225–227, 308–309), then mark 228 ready to activate only after this gate is green.

source: Steward conclusion: beta integrity gate before learner pilot 228; not another product feature pass
